rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in the correct AADL scope: component type for interface assumptions/guarantees, implementation for bindings.
Implementation-level signal routing contracts should be placed in the implementation annex after relevant subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by placing AGREE contracts in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope to avoid unresolved reference errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for interface-level assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or vice versa.
Do not reference subcomponent ports or data types not visible in the current scope.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assume/guarantee syntax and validator-driven repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
